question: Which of the following is NOT one of the DMN data types Camunda supports?

a.text: False / not supported, so this is the correct answer for a NOT question. The DMN data-types page lists `number` as the single numeric type — there is no separate `integer` (or `long`, `decimal`) type in Camunda DMN.

b.text: True. `dayTimeDuration` is one of the supported DMN types, mapped to the FEEL Days-Time-Duration type.

c.text: True. `Any` is listed as a wildcard data type that accepts a value of any type.

d.text: True. `dateTime` is one of the supported DMN types, mapped to the FEEL Date-Time type.

explanation: Camunda's DMN data-types table lists number, string, boolean, time, date, dateTime, dayTimeDuration, yearMonthDuration, and Any. There is no separate integer type — numeric values use number. Authors coming from typed languages often expect a distinct integer type and mis-type columns as a result.
